title: dodecatheon meadia var. frigidum
intro: Dodecatheon meadia var. frigidum, also known as the Rocky Mountain shooting star, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to possess antiseptic and antiviral capabilities. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Dodecatheon meadia var. frigidum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
